title: Heterotheca mariana
intro: Heterotheca mariana, also known as Maryland aster or Mary land mariana, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to eastern North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects, which can aid in the relief of muscle cramps, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Heterotheca mariana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Heterotheca mariana is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Heterotheca mariana is classified within the plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf arrangement. Within the Asteraceae family, Heterotheca mariana is further classified in the tribe Heliantheae and the subtribe Heterothecinae. It is a member of the genus Heterotheca, which is a group of plants native to North America. The exact classification of Heterotheca mariana can be found in the most recent editions of botanical classification systems.
